title: P1: Reboot --wait can return false positive (race condition)
description:
Reboot --wait calls wait_for_state(Running), which polls getMachine until state is running. If the instance was already running before the reboot API call is processed, wait_for_state returns immediately even though the reboot hasn't happened yet. node-triton uses waitForMachineAudit to check the audit trail for a successful reboot action, which is definitive.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/wait.rs:73-99
Comparison: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/lifecycle.rs:104-126 vs target/node-triton/lib/do_instance_reboot.js

Fix: For reboot specifically, record a timestamp before the action and verify via audit trail or by checking that the machine transitioned through a non-running state.
close_reason: Fixed: reboot --wait now uses audit-trail polling instead of ambiguous state==running check

title: P1: triton env --docker silently empty when docker not set up

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs:197-202
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_env.js:121-126

Fix: When --docker is explicitly passed and setup.json is missing, return an error with setup instructions.
Test: Test triton env --docker with no setup.json.

title: Missing serde rename overrides on CreateMachineRequest fields
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs, CreateMachineRequest struct (line 320, rename_all = "camelCase")

Four fields lack explicit serde rename overrides but the Node.js wire format uses snake_case:

1. firewall_enabled (line 355) - auto-renames to firewallEnabled, wire uses firewall_enabled
2. deletion_protection (line 358) - auto-renames to deletionProtection, wire uses deletion_protection
3. delegate_dataset (line 372) - auto-renames to delegateDataset, wire uses delegate_dataset
4. allow_shared_images (line 378) - auto-renames to allowSharedImages, wire uses allow_shared_images

Evidence: The Machine response struct already has explicit renames for firewall_enabled, deletion_protection,
and delegate_dataset, confirming the Node.js API uses snake_case for these fields.

Critical: CreateMachineRequest has #[serde(flatten)] extra which would silently swallow the snake_case
fields from node-triton into the HashMap rather than raising an error.

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "...")] with snake_case names to each field:
  #[serde(rename = "firewall_enabled")]
  pub firewall_enabled: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "deletion_protection")]
  pub deletion_protection: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "delegate_dataset")]
  pub delegate_dataset: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "allow_shared_images")]
  pub allow_shared_images: Option<bool>,

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check

title: HTTP 410 Gone handling bypasses Progenitor client with 70 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 551-620

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns HTTP 410 Gone with a Machine body (not an error) for deleted machines. Progenitor treats 410 as an error and fails to parse. Workaround: 70 lines of manual HTTP handling that bypasses Progenitor entirely, including:
- Manual `get_machine()` method with direct reqwest usage
- Custom `GetMachineError` enum with `NotFound` variant
- Bypasses Progenitor response parsing

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor response override capabilities. May require upstream feature request for non-error 4xx body handling.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — deleted machine queries should still work.
close_reason: Fixed: wrap Progenitor call, catch InvalidResponsePayload for 410 recovery

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Fixed by wrapping Progenitor get_machine() and catching InvalidResponsePayload (which surfaces when 410 Gone body can't be deserialized as Error). Reduced from 70 lines of manual HTTP/auth code to a 24-line match on the Progenitor error. Also removed cloudapi-cli (dead code, superseded by triton-cli) and switched all CLI callers to use the typed wrapper.

title: Exit code 3 for not-found errors not implemented
description:
## Summary

Node.js triton uses exit code 3 for "resource not found" errors (documented
in help text). Rust only uses 0, 1, 2 (where 2 is clap usage errors).

This may affect scripts that check exit codes to distinguish "error" from
"not found".

## Fix

When an API call returns 404 or a resource lookup fails, exit with code 3
instead of 1. This requires catching the specific error type before it
reaches the generic anyhow error handler in main.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs` — error handling / exit code logic
- May need a custom error type or match on HTTP status codes

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_error_paths.rs`
- Assert `triton instance get nonexistent-uuid` exits with code 3
- Assert other errors still exit with code 1

Differences.md issue #16.

title: Image cache save_list silently drops serialization failures
description: cli/triton-cli/src/cache.rs:52-57 — serde_json::to_string(images).unwrap_or_default() converts serialization failures into empty strings written to the cache file. If an Image type has a serde bug, the cache perpetually fails with no diagnostic. Also, 'let _ = tokio::fs::write(...)' silently drops file-write errors. Fix: use tracing::debug logging instead of unwrap_or_default(), and log write errors. The module is intentionally silent (matching node-triton), but debug-level logging would help troubleshooting.

title: triton env missing --triton/--docker/--smartdc/--unset flags
description:
## Summary

Node.js `triton env` supports flags to control which sections are emitted:
- `-t/--triton` — emit only triton section
- `-d/--docker` — emit only docker section
- `-s/--smartdc` — emit only smartdc section
- `-u/--unset` — emit unset commands instead of export

Rust only has `-s/--shell` to choose shell type (bash/fish). All sections
are always emitted and there is no unset mode.

## Fix

Add the missing flags to the env command. The `-s` flag conflicts — Node
uses `-s` for `--smartdc` while Rust uses `-s` for `--shell`. Need to
decide on flag mapping. Suggest using `--shell` (long only) for shell
type to free up `-s` for `--smartdc` compatibility.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs`

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs` (create if needed)
- Assert `--triton` emits only the triton section
- Assert `--unset` emits `unset` commands

Differences.md issue #11.

description:
Three locations in triton-auth silently discard errors, making debugging difficult:

1. libs/triton-auth/src/lib.rs:218-230 — KeySource::Auto catches Err(_) from SSH agent and falls back to file keys. Users with agent problems get misleading 'key not found in ~/.ssh/' errors. Should distinguish KeyNotFound (expected) from other agent errors (unexpected).

2. libs/triton-auth/src/agent.rs:82-86 — list_agent_keys_sync uses 'if let Ok(pub_key) = parse_public_key_from_bytes(...)' to silently skip unparseable keys. Keys using unsupported algorithms vanish from listings with no indication.

3. libs/triton-auth/src/agent.rs:92-96 — is_agent_available() uses .unwrap_or(false) on spawn_blocking result, swallowing task panics as 'agent not available'.

Fix: Add tracing::debug logging before each fallback so --verbose reveals the real cause. For #1, also distinguish error types.

title: Field naming exceptions undocumented in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not document the conventions around field naming exceptions (e.g., when serde rename attributes override the default naming). Contributors may not know when/how to use rename attributes. Fix: add a section documenting field naming conventions and exception patterns.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: Untyped String fields that should be enums: volume_type, VolumeMount.mode, ImageRequirements.brand
description:
Several fields use raw String where typed enums would provide type safety:

1. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs:50 — Volume.volume_type is String. Known type is 'tritonnfs'. Should be a VolumeType enum with Tritonnfs + #[serde(other)] Unknown.

2. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs:234 — VolumeMount.mode is Option<String>. Values are 'rw' or 'ro'. Should be a MountMode enum.

3. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:76 — ImageRequirements.brand is Option<String>. Should be Option<VmapiBrand> since it maps directly to the Brand enum.

Fix: Create typed enums for each, following the existing #[serde(other)] Unknown pattern.

title: MigrationPhase enum missing #[serde(other)] catch-all
description: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/migrations.rs:87-93 — MigrationPhase only has 3 variants with no catch-all. A new migration phase from the server will cause deserialization failure. Fix: add #[serde(other)] Unknown variant.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From reviews 2C/3A: vmapi-api types and type-safety audit. Rated HIGH — forward compat.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on VmState in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 779, 808, 1525

VmState enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string. VmState has a Display impl in apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs, but the CLI should use enum_to_display() for consistency with triton-cli.

Examples:
- Line 779: `println!("{}: {} ({:?})", vm.uuid, alias_str, vm.state);`
- Line 808: `println!("State: {:?}", vm.state);`
- Line 1525: `println!("  {}: {:?}", uuid, status.state);`

Debug format produces "Running" while wire format is "running". This is inconsistent with triton-cli output.

Suggested fix: Use enum_to_display() from triton-cli output module, or add a similar helper to vmapi-cli:
```rust
println!("{}: {} ({})", vm.uuid, alias_str, enum_to_display(&vm.state));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: 410 Gone recovery catches all InvalidResponsePayload errors
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:525-529 - The get_machine error handler catches InvalidResponsePayload which does not carry HTTP status code. It fires for any response failing to parse, not just 410. Low practical risk but comment is misleading.

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on ImageState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs, lines 27-33

ImageState enum is deserialized from server responses (used in Image struct at line 143) but lacks a #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. If the server adds a new image state, deserialization will fail.

Current definition:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
}
```

Suggested fix: Add an Unknown catch-all variant:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
    /// Unknown state (forward compatibility)
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

Note: ImageState also has clap::ValueEnum derived. The Unknown variant will need #[clap(skip)] to prevent it from appearing as a CLI argument option.

Verification: Run `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` and `make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` to confirm compilation and tests pass.

title: Inconsistent image action dispatch: query param vs body
description:
In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, image action methods use inconsistent approaches for sending the action parameter:

- export_image (line 1296): uses ActionBody to put action in body
- update_image_metadata (line 1277): uses .action() query parameter
- clone_image (line 1327): uses .action() query parameter
- import_image_from_datacenter (line 1352): uses .action() query parameter

All machine/volume/disk actions consistently use ActionBody. The image methods diverge without comments explaining why. Either standardize on one approach or add per-method comments documenting the wire-format rationale (e.g. clone_image has a comment about matching node-triton, but export_image has none explaining its different approach).

title: TableBuilder::add_row() does not validate row length
close_reason: wontfix: add_row() is no longer used in production code — all table construction now goes through from_columns() and from_enum_columns(), which build rows internally with correct lengths. The only remaining add_row() call site is the test helper sample_builder(). Adding a debug_assert to protect a test-only path isn't worth the churn.

title: SshIdentity::new public API lacks bounds validation
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:123-139 — SshIdentity::new is a public API that accepts raw bytes but does not validate bounds or format. Callers can create malformed identities that cause panics later during use. Fix: add validation in the constructor.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on slog::Level in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 76

This is the canonical anti-pattern: `format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()` — formatting an enum with Debug and calling .to_lowercase().

Code:
```rust
format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()
```

slog::Level is an external library type so enum_to_display() is not directly applicable. However, the pattern is fragile — Debug output format is not guaranteed stable.

Suggested fix: Use slog::Level display string directly if available, or define a const for the default level name string.

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver`.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on Brand in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, line 807

Brand enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string.

Code: `println!("Brand: {:?}", vm.brand);`

Debug format produces "Bhyve" while wire format is "bhyve" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("Brand: {}", enum_to_display(&vm.brand));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify brand output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: CLI compat: image copy semantics differ from node-triton
description:
payload-image-copy test shows fundamental semantic difference:
- Node does GET image + GET datacenters, then exits 1 (image copy is not fully implemented in emit-payload mode)
- Rust sends POST with import-from-datacenter action directly, exits 0
- Need to decide: match Node's incomplete behavior, or keep Rust's correct-but-different approach
- May require updating the node-triton emit-payload patch to handle image copy properly

title: triton image copy missing 'cp' alias
description:
Comparison test ID: help-image

Node.js 'triton image' has 'copy (cp)' but Rust only has 'copy' without the 'cp' alias.
'triton image cp' fails with 'unrecognized subcommand'.

Fix: add visible_alias = "cp" to the copy subcommand in the image command.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs (copy subcommand definition)
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs

title: Package.flexible_disk silently drops data due to serde rename mismatch
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs:54,91-92 — Package struct has #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] so serde expects flexibleDisk, but CloudAPI sends flexible_disk (snake_case). Since the field has #[serde(default)], it silently becomes None even when the API returns true. Existing tests never assert on this field. Fix: add #[serde(rename = "flexible_disk")] override on the field and add test assertion.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman

title: Certgen produces self-signed certs instead of SSH-key-signed
description: libs/triton-auth/src/certgen.rs:97-100 — Certificate generation produces self-signed certificates rather than certificates signed with the SSH key. This is a functional gap that limits the usefulness of the certgen module. Fix: implement proper SSH key signing for generated certificates.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM — functional gap.

title: resolve_instance does not check for ambiguous short-IDs
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:67-80 - Returns first prefix match without checking for duplicates. If two machines share the same 8-char UUID prefix, the wrong machine could be silently operated on. Should collect all matches and error if ambiguous.

title: Changefeed WebSocket command tests
description: The changefeed command is WebSocket-based and has zero test coverage. Testing would require a mock WebSocket server or a dedicated test fixture. Consider adding at minimum help/existence tests and optionally a mock-server integration test.

title: Instance audit command tests
description: The instance audit command has no tests - not even help/existence tests in cli_subcommands.rs. Add help tests and API integration tests verifying the audit trail endpoint returns expected format.

title: Action-dispatch pattern missing from AGENTS.md
description: The action-dispatch CLI pattern (commands dispatching to sub-actions) is only documented in skill reference files, not in AGENTS.md. This pattern is central to the CLI architecture but invisible to contributors reading the main guide. Fix: add a section to AGENTS.md documenting the action-dispatch pattern.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated HIGH — key architectural pattern undocumented.

title: CLI compat: image action wire format (clone, share, unshare)
description: node-triton sends image actions via query param (?action=clone, ?action=update with acl array for share). Rust CLI sends action in the request body using the action-dispatch pattern. Also node-triton's unshare fails (exit 1) in emit-payload mode. Affects payload tests: payload-image-clone, payload-image-share, payload-image-unshare.
close_reason: Fixed across 4edc042 and 6c51b8a: image actions now use query param wire format, shared fixtures resolve all diffs

title: Add positional key=value filter support to image list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton image list` (e.g., `triton images name=base-64`). The Rust CLI currently only supports --flag style filters.

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]` to `ImageListArgs`
2. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into existing args fields
3. Use `parse_serde_enum()` helper for enum values (state, type), `.parse()` for primitives
4. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_image/do_list.js`): name, os, version, public, state, owner, type

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: --name, --version, --os, --state (ImageState), --type/--image-type (ImageType), --public, --all

title: P2: cli_subcommands.rs tests only check non-empty output (218 weak assertions)
description:
218 tests (~34% of all tests) only assert .stdout(predicate::str::is_empty().not()) — verifies something was printed but not correctness. These pass even if help output is garbage.

File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_subcommands.rs

Fix: Add predicate::str::contains("Usage:") to help-output tests.

title: Hardcoded enum string "stopped"/"running"/"deleted" in cli_manage_workflow.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_manage_workflow.rs
Lines: 370, 385, 404, 530

**Current code:**
- Line 370: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "stopped", ...)`
- Line 385: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "running", ...)`
- Line 404: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "running", ...)`
- Line 530: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "deleted", ...)`

**Problem:** Test assertions compare against hardcoded string literals instead of typed `MachineState` enum variants. If variant serde names change, tests silently pass with wrong comparisons.

**Suggested fix:** Change test struct `state` field from `String` to `MachineState`, or compare against `enum_to_display(MachineState::Running)` etc.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — tests should still pass with typed comparisons.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced hardcoded state strings with MachineState enum variants

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on VmState
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`
**Has:** Custom `Display` and `FromStr` implementations

**Problem:** `VmState` is used in vmapi-cli as a value_enum argument (vmapi-cli/src/main.rs:60, 86) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli`

title: Missing serde tests for User/Role/Policy, machine_resources, and migration types
description:
Several type modules in apis/cloudapi-api lack serde round-trip tests:

1. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/user.rs — User, Role, and Policy all use #[serde(rename = "role-tag")] on their role_tag fields. Given how many bugs this pattern has caused elsewhere, this is a significant gap.

2. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs — Snapshot, Disk, SnapshotState, DiskState have no serde tests. The #[serde(other)] Unknown forward compatibility is never verified.

3. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs — Migration, MigrationState, MigrationPhase have no serde tests. The Migration.vm_uuid field uses #[serde(alias = "machine")] which is a tricky alias pattern.

Fix: Add serde_user.rs, serde_machine_resources.rs, and serde_migration.rs test files with fixture JSON, following the existing pattern in serde_machine.rs.

title: update_access_key returns HTTP 201 instead of 200
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/lib.rs:1707,1790 - update_access_key and update_user_access_key use HttpResponseCreated (201) instead of HttpResponseOk (200). Update operations should return 200. All other update endpoints correctly use HttpResponseOk.

title: triton volume create: node-triton rejects size 10240 client-side
description:
Comparison test IDs: payload-volume-create, payload-volume-create-net

Node.js triton validates volume sizes client-side and rejects 10240 as invalid: 'size "10240" is not a valid volume size'. Rust triton passes the size through to the API.

This may indicate Rust should add client-side size validation, or the comparison test needs to use a size that node-triton accepts.

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_volumes.rs

title: triton network create: camelCase vs snake_case field names in payload
description:
Comparison test ID: payload-network-create-gw

When creating a fabric network with --gateway, Rust uses camelCase field names while Node.js uses snake_case:
- Node: provision_start_ip, provision_end_ip
- Rust: provisionStartIp, provisionEndIp

Node also sends empty 'resolvers': [] and 'routes': {} that Rust omits.

Diff:
-    "provision_end_ip": "10.0.0.254",
-    "provision_start_ip": "10.0.0.1",
-    "resolvers": [],
-    "routes": {},
+    "provisionEndIp": "10.0.0.254",
+    "provisionStartIp": "10.0.0.1",

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/network.rs (and potentially apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/ for the struct definition)
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_networks.rs

title: triton env --shell fish produces output where Node.js fails
description:
Comparison test ID: env-fish

Node.js exits 1 (does not support fish shell), while Rust exits 0 and produces valid fish shell output. This is technically an improvement in the Rust CLI.

Node.js: exit code 1, no output
Rust:    exit code 0, produces valid fish set -gx commands

This may be intentional. Consider whether to mark as ignored-diff or keep tracking.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs (create if needed)
close_reason: Intentional: Rust adds fish shell support that Node.js lacks

title: Debug format anti-pattern: format!("{:?}", DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase() in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 76

Current code: format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()

This is the exact documented anti-pattern: using Debug format on an enum and then lowercasing. It relies on the Debug representation of slog::Level happening to produce the desired output.

Fix: slog::Level implements Display (which produces the lowercase string directly). Replace with:
  format!("{}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL) or util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL.to_string()

Verification: Build with make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver and confirm --help output still shows the correct default log level.

title: Debug format {:?} in dry-run output for Brand enum
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/create.rs:236-254 — Brand enum is printed with Debug format {:?} in dry-run output, showing Rust debug representation instead of wire-format string. Fix: use enum_to_display() or Display trait for user-facing output.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated HIGH — Debug format anti-pattern.

title: AccessKey.status and credentialtype are bare Strings instead of typed enums
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/key.rs:72-89 — AccessKey struct uses bare String for status and credentialtype fields instead of typed enums. This loses type safety and forces string comparisons in consuming code. Fix: define proper enums with #[serde(other)] Unknown variants for both fields.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2B: cloudapi-api types review. Rated HIGH — type safety gap.

title: Certificate loading silently drops individual certs in root store
description: cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs:326-327, 362-363, 380-381 — 'let _ = root_store.add(cert)' silently ignores errors when adding certificates to the TLS root store. If a user's CA bundle contains a malformed cert, it is silently skipped. Later TLS failures give no hint that a cert failed to load. Fix: count and log skipped certificates at tracing::debug level.

title: Divergent profile resolution logic between config/mod.rs and main.rs
description: cli/triton-cli/src/config/mod.rs and cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs — Profile resolution logic is duplicated between these two files with subtle differences. This can lead to different profiles being selected depending on code path. Fix: consolidate into a single resolution function.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3C: triton-cli infrastructure commands review. Rated MEDIUM — divergent logic.

title: Inconsistent enum_to_display() vs Display usage in migration.rs
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/migration.rs - Uses {} (Display) in 10 places and enum_to_display() in 6 places within the same file. Both produce correct output but AGENTS.md says to use enum_to_display() for serde enums.

title: triton network create: missing --gateway/--no-nat validation
description:
Comparison test ID: payload-network-create

Node.js triton requires either --gateway (-g) or --no-nat (-x) when creating a fabric network. Rust triton accepts the command without either flag and emits the payload.

Node error: 'without a --gateway (-g), you must specify --no-nat (-x)'
Rust: emits POST /fabrics/default/vlans/100/networks payload successfully

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/network.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_networks.rs

title: Hardcoded enum string "failed" in rbac/apply.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs, line 797

**Current code:**
- `status: "failed".to_string()` in `ApplyChangeResult`

**Problem:** The `status` field in `ApplyChangeResult` is a `String` that takes values "ok", "failed", "skipped". These should be a typed enum.

**Suggested fix:** Define an `ApplyStatus` enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum ApplyStatus {
    Ok,
    Failed,
    Skipped,
}
```
Then change `ApplyChangeResult.status` from `String` to `ApplyStatus`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Replaced hardcoded status strings with ApplyStatus enum

title: Debug format in UnexpectedRecovery error variant reaches users
description: In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:1031, the UnexpectedRecovery error uses {state:?} Debug format which can reach end users, violating Type Safety Rule 7. Should use enum_to_display or Display format instead of Debug.

title: SSH agent parsing panics on malformed responses
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:46-258 — read_u32be, read_u8, read_string do unchecked array indexing. A malformed SSH agent response causes panics instead of returning errors. Fix: use checked slicing with proper error returns.

title: RBAC role query param added after request signing

If the server validates the full URI including query string against the signature, this causes a signature mismatch. Fix: add the role query param before signing the request.

title: Debug format on user-facing println: "Added msg: {:?}" in manatee-echo-resolver

Disk commands (list, get, resize, delete) have zero offline behavioral tests. Only 2 help-output tests exist.

title: resolve_instance short-ID lookup does not verify hex input

title: Hardcoded enum string "tritonnfs" in volume.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs, line 86
The clap default_value uses hardcoded string "tritonnfs" matching VolumeType::Tritonnfs wire name.

Current code:
    #[arg(long, short = 't', default_value = "tritonnfs")]

This pattern is already used elsewhere (e.g., default_value_t = DEFAULT_CERT_LIFETIME_DAYS in profile.rs:106).

title: Missing serde rename override on default_network in Config/UpdateConfigRequest
description:

- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/account.rs, line 126 (UpdateConfigRequest struct, line 122)

Both structs have rename_all = "camelCase" but default_network should be snake_case on the wire.

Actual wire format: default_network (confirmed by Node.js translateUfdsConf returning default_network)

title: json_stream_parse silently drops parse failures
description: cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:83-88 — json_stream_parse uses filter_map to silently drop lines that fail JSON parsing. This means test assertions can pass even when the CLI output is malformed. Fix: fail the test if any line fails to parse, or at minimum log warnings.

title: Missing forward-compat tests for 11 enums with #[serde(other)]

Missing: MachineType, SnapshotState (cloudapi), DiskState, ChangefeedResource, ChangefeedSubResource, MigrationState (cloudapi misc), MigrationPhase (cloudapi misc), VmAction (vmapi), SnapshotState (vmapi). 13 other enums DO have these tests.

title: triton get commands: match Node.js JSON indent and key ordering
description:
Comparison test IDs: image-get, package-get, network-get
- Rust uses 2-space indent, Node uses 4-space indent for JSON get output

title: profile set-current fails for env profile
description:
## Summary

Node.js: `triton profile set-current env` → `"env" is already the current profile`
Rust: `triton profile set-current env` → `Error: Failed to read profile 'env': No such file or directory`

The "env" profile is constructed from environment variables (TRITON_URL, etc.)
and has no file in `~/.triton/profiles.d/`. The Rust `set_current_profile()`
calls `Profile::load(name)` which tries to read from disk and fails.

## Fix

`set_current_profile()` should use `resolve_profile(Some(name))` instead of
`Profile::load(name)` to handle the "env" profile, or special-case "env"
when the relevant environment variables are set.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs` — `set_current_profile()` (around line 487-505)

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_profiles.rs`
- Set TRITON_URL/TRITON_ACCOUNT/TRITON_KEY_ID env vars
- Assert `triton profile set-current env` succeeds

Differences.md issue #4.

title: load_from_common_paths skips PKCS#1/legacy keys during auto-discovery
description:
## Summary

`KeyLoader::load_from_common_paths()` in `libs/triton-auth/src/key_loader.rs:225-258`
only calls `load_from_file()` for each candidate key in `~/.ssh/`. That method rejects
non-OpenSSH formats (PKCS#1, SEC1, DSA, PKCS#8) with an error. It never falls back to
`load_legacy_from_file()`, which supports all these formats.

This means any user whose `~/.ssh/id_rsa` is in PKCS#1 PEM format
(`-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----`) — which is common for older keys — gets:

    Error: Error: Key not found: No key with fingerprint ... found in ~/.ssh/

Node.js triton loads these keys successfully.

This is differences.md issue #1 and blocks all API commands for affected users.

## Root cause

`load_from_common_paths()` line 240:
```rust
if let Ok(key) = Self::load_from_file(&path, None).await {
    if fingerprint.matches(key.public_key()) {
        return Ok(key);
    }
}
```

When `load_from_file` returns Err for a PKCS#1 key, the loop silently continues
to the next file. It never tries `load_legacy_from_file` as a fallback.

## Fix

After `load_from_file` fails, try `load_legacy_from_file` before moving on.
The challenge is that `load_from_file` returns `PrivateKey` (ssh_key crate)
while `load_legacy_from_file` returns `LegacyPrivateKey`. The caller
(`load_private_key` via `KeySource::Auto`) would need to handle both, or
`load_from_common_paths` needs a new return type / the two key types need
unification.

Additionally, `load_from_common_paths` only checks 4 hardcoded filenames
(`id_ed25519`, `id_ecdsa`, `id_rsa`, `id_dsa`). Node.js triton scans all
files in `~/.ssh/` by fingerprint. This is a secondary issue but worth
noting.

## Integration test to add

File: `libs/triton-auth/tests/fs_keys_test.rs`

The existing tests all call `load_legacy_from_file` directly. There is no
test for `load_from_common_paths` or the `KeySource::Auto` code path. Add:

1. **Test: PKCS#1 RSA key discovered via `load_from_common_paths`**
   - Create a temp dir as fake `$HOME`
   - Place the existing test PKCS#1 key (`tests/keys/id_rsa`) at `$HOME/.ssh/id_rsa`
   - Compute its fingerprint (known: `fa:56:a1:6b:cc:04:97:fe:e2:98:54:c4:2e:0d:26:c6`)
   - Call `load_from_common_paths` (or `load_private_key` with `KeySource::Auto`)
     with that fingerprint
   - Assert it succeeds and the key type is RSA

2. **Test: ECDSA SEC1 key discovered via `load_from_common_paths`**
   - Same pattern with `tests/keys/id_ecdsa` (fingerprint: `00:74:32:ae:0a:24:3c:7a:e7:07:b8:ee:91:c4:c7:27`)

3. **Test: OpenSSH format key still works** (regression guard)
   - Generate or provide an OpenSSH-format ed25519 key
   - Verify `load_from_common_paths` finds it

Note: `load_from_common_paths` is `async fn` (private). It may need to be
made `pub(crate)` for direct testing, or tests can go through
`load_private_key(KeySource::Auto { .. })` which is the public entry point.
The `Auto` path tries the SSH agent first and falls back to
`load_from_common_paths`, so the test environment should ensure no SSH agent
is running (or `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is unset).

## Existing test keys available

- `tests/keys/id_rsa` — PKCS#1 PEM format, MD5: `fa:56:a1:6b:cc:04:97:fe:e2:98:54:c4:2e:0d:26:c6`
- `tests/keys/id_ecdsa` — SEC1 PEM format, MD5: `00:74:32:ae:0a:24:3c:7a:e7:07:b8:ee:91:c4:c7:27`
- `tests/keys/id_dsa` — DSA PEM format, MD5: `a6:e6:68:d3:28:2b:0a:a0:12:54:da:c4:c0:22:8d:ba`

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:58-67 (RbacConfigUser struct)
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/rbac.js:259-335 (loadUserKeys), :443-533 (key CRUD in plan)

Test: Test apply with user key configuration.

title: Add positional key=value filter support to network list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton network list` (e.g., `triton networks public=true`). The Rust CLI currently only supports --flag style filters.

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]` to `NetworkListArgs`
2. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into existing args fields
3. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_network/do_list.js`): public

**Note**: This is a small change since there is only one filter key. The filtering is client-side in node-triton (filters after fetching all networks).

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/network.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: --public (bool)

title: No serde tests for MemberRef, PolicyRef, MemberType RBAC types
description:
The new structured RBAC types in apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/user.rs have no serde serialization/deserialization tests:

- MemberRef with #[serde(rename = "type")] on member_type field
- PolicyRef with optional name and id fields
- MemberType with #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]

These replace the previous Vec<String> fields in CreateRoleRequest/UpdateRoleRequest, making this a wire-format breaking change for CloudAPI v9.0.0+. If the serde rename on member_type broke (serializing as "member_type" instead of "type"), role creation would silently fail server-side. Add roundtrip tests in apis/cloudapi-api/tests/serde_actions.rs or a new serde_user.rs.

title: UUID handling behavior change undocumented in AGENTS.md

title: CreateMachine legacy format transformation is 152 lines of manual code

File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 1109-1260

- Two separate methods: `create_machine()` and `create_machine_from_progenitor()`

title: triton env missing Docker environment variables
description:
## Summary

When docker-setup has been run, Node.js `triton env` outputs Docker vars:

    # docker
    export DOCKER_CERT_PATH=/Users/user/.triton/docker/...
    export DOCKER_HOST=tcp://us-central-1.docker.mnx.io:2376
    export DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY=1
    export COMPOSE_HTTP_TIMEOUT=300

Rust always outputs an empty Docker section:

    # docker

This breaks the Docker setup workflow via `eval "$(triton env)"`.

## Fix

Check for docker cert/host configuration in the profile's docker setup
directory and emit the appropriate environment variables when present.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs`

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs` (create if needed)
- Create a fake docker setup directory with certs
- Assert `triton env` outputs DOCKER_* variables

Differences.md issue #9.

title: Add positional key=value filter support to package list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton package list` (e.g., `triton packages memory=1024`). The Rust CLI currently only supports --flag style filters.

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]` to `PackageListArgs`
2. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into existing args fields
3. Use `.parse()` for numeric values (memory, disk, swap, lwps, vcpus)
4. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_package/do_list.js`): name, memory, disk, swap, lwps, version, vcpus, group

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/package.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: --name, --memory, --disk (note: some node-triton filters like swap, lwps, vcpus, group may need new --flag fields too)

title: P1: rbac info --all silently swallows key fetch errors
description:
In rbac info --all, individual key fetch failures are silently swallowed: `if let Ok(keys) = keys_result`. A failed key fetch for one user silently results in that user appearing to have 0 keys with no warning or error output. This could mislead operators into thinking a user has no keys when actually the API call failed.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:237-239

Fix: Log a warning when key fetch fails, or include an error indicator in the output.
Test: Mock server returning 500 for one user's keys.

title: Missing serde tests for types with tricky rename attributes
description:
Several CloudAPI types with non-trivial serde configurations lack round-trip deserialization tests:

1. FirewallRule (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/firewall.rs) — has #[serde(rename = "role-tag")] hyphenated rename
2. SshKey (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/key.rs) — also has #[serde(rename = "role-tag")]
3. Package and DiskSize (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs) — DiskSize is #[serde(untagged)] which is fragile with serde (variant ordering matters)
4. Changefeed types (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs) — multiple rename strategies (rename_all = "lowercase", rename_all = "snake_case") and the canonical #[serde(other)] pattern

The commit history shows serde rename bugs are real (multiple commits fix snake_case vs camelCase issues). Add fixture-based serde round-trip tests for these types.
close_reason: Added serde tests for FirewallRule (role-tag), SshKey (role-tag), Package (role-tag + flexible_disk), and DiskSize (untagged enum).

**Suggested fix:** Change derives to `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq, clap::ValueEnum`.

title: Double Error prefix in all API error messages
description:
## Summary

All API error messages show "Error: Error: ..." due to double-wrapping.

Node.js: `triton: error (SomeCode): message`
Rust: `Error: Error: Key not found: No key with fingerprint ...`

## Root cause

Progenitor's `Error::Custom` variant's `Display` impl adds an `"Error: "` prefix
(`write!(f, "Error: {}", s)`). Then anyhow adds another `"Error: "` when `main()`
returns `Err`.

## Fix

Either strip the `Error::Custom` wrapping before returning from main, or use a
custom error display that doesn't double-prefix. Could also consider matching
Node.js format: `triton: error (Code): message`.

## Files
- CLI main error handling (likely `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs`)
- Wherever progenitor errors are wrapped/displayed

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_api_errors.rs` or `cli_error_paths.rs`
- Assert error messages don't contain "Error: Error:"

Differences.md issue #2.

title: CLI compat: fwrule enable/disable wire format
description: node-triton sends POST /fwrules/{id}/enable with empty body. Rust CLI sends POST /fwrules/{id} with {enabled: true/false} in body. The Rust approach uses the update endpoint instead of dedicated enable/disable paths. Affects payload tests: payload-fwrule-enable, payload-fwrule-disable.

title: Mixed TLS backends (reqwest=rustls, tokio-tungstenite=native-tls)
description: Workspace uses rustls for reqwest but native-tls for tokio-tungstenite. This means two TLS implementations are compiled and linked, increasing binary size and attack surface. Fix: unify on a single TLS backend (preferably rustls) across all crates.

title: Add automatic pagination (LOMStream equivalent) to cloudapi-client
description: The Rust CLI makes single requests for list endpoints and returns whatever the server gives back. node-triton uses LOMStream which automatically pages through results in 1000-item chunks (limit+offset) until exhausted. Without client-side pagination, users with >1000 machines/images/etc get silently truncated results. The Progenitor-generated builders already accept .limit() and .offset(), so the plumbing exists — we need a pagination helper in cloudapi-client that wraps list endpoints and loops until the response has fewer items than the limit.

title: Top-level aliases differ from Node.js (ls, instances, rm)
description:
## Summary

Node.js triton has top-level shortcut commands that Rust implements differently:

| Alias       | Node.js                    | Rust                        |
|-------------|----------------------------|-----------------------------|
| ls          | Shortcut for instance list | Alias on insts (works)      |
| instances   | Shortcut for instance list | Alias on insts (works)      |
| rm          | Shortcut for instance delete | Alias on delete (works)   |

The Rust versions work but are implemented as aliases on different commands,
which may cause minor behavioral differences (help text, error messages).

Additionally, Rust adds `svcs` as an alias for `services` which Node.js
doesn't have (Node returns "unknown command" for svcs). This is additive
and not a bug.

## Fix

Verify that `ls`, `instances`, and `rm` produce identical output to their
Node.js equivalents. If there are differences in behavior (not just help
text), fix them. If behavior matches, this can be closed as compatible.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs` or wherever top-level commands are defined

## Test
- Comparison framework alias tests (API tier) already cover `insts`, `imgs`,
  `pkgs`, `nets`. Could add `ls` and `rm` alias tests.

Differences.md issue #13.
close_reason: compatible: ls, instances, and rm aliases all route to the exact same handler functions (instance::list::run and instance::delete::run) with identical argument structs. The only difference is help text, which is expected for top-level shortcuts vs nested subcommands. 225 subcommand alias tests pass confirming all aliases work correctly.

title: Fingerprint::matches returns false on key serialization failure
description:
libs/triton-auth/src/fingerprint.rs:89-93 — The matches() method catches PublicKey::to_bytes() errors and returns false. If a key fails to serialize (corrupted or unsupported type like certificate keys), the method silently reports 'no match' instead of surfacing the error. User sees 'Key not found' instead of the real serialization error, misdirecting debugging effort.

Fix: Add tracing::warn on the Err branch: tracing::warn!("Failed to serialize public key for fingerprint matching: {}", e)

title: P2: triton env drops null docker env values silently
description:
node-triton emits 'unset KEY' for null docker env values. Rust's filter_map skips null values entirely — no unset command generated. If docker setup.json has DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY: null, the environment variable won't be cleaned up.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs:37
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_env.js:105-111

Fix: Handle null values by emitting unset commands.
Test: Test with fixture containing null docker env values.

title: CLI compat: NIC add/remove wire format differences

Two NIC-related payload comparison failures:
1. payload-nic-add: Rust omits the default 'primary': false field that Node includes. Also exit code differs (node=1, rust=0).
2. payload-nic-remove: Node strips colons from MAC address in URL path (aabbccddeeff), Rust preserves them (aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff). Node's behavior matches CloudAPI expectation.
Fix: Add primary:false default to NIC add payload, strip colons from MAC in NIC remove URL path.

title: P2: Debug format {:?} in user-facing --show-headers output

cloudapi.rs uses Debug format for HTTP version in --show-headers output: println!("{:?} {}", response.version(), response.status()). Shows HTTP/1.1 as HTTP11 or similar Debug representation.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/cloudapi.rs:159

Fix: Use Display trait or manual formatting for HTTP version.

title: RBAC role edit silently drops malformed PolicyRef/MemberRef entries

The user believes they added a policy/member but it was discarded. Should at minimum eprintln a warning for each unparseable entry, or collect errors and report them.

title: ExportImageRequest has redundant rename_all = camelCase

The struct-level attribute is harmless but misleading -- a reader might think the wire format is camelCase when it's actually snake_case. title: NIC netmask_to_cidr silently skips unparseable octets
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs:127-133, filter_map with .ok() silently skips unparseable netmask octets, producing wrong CIDR values. For 255.abc.255.0, it computes CIDR from only 3 octets. Should validate exactly 4 valid octets or return Option<u8> and display ? on failure.

title: GetMachineError::Client flattens all errors to String
description:
In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:1011, the catch-all arm of get_machine converts Progenitor errors to String via e.to_string(), discarding the error variant and source chain:

    Err(e) => Err(GetMachineError::Client(e.to_string()))

This handles CommunicationError, InvalidRequest, PreHookError, etc. Downstream code cannot distinguish network failures from auth failures from server errors. Consider wrapping the original Error<types::Error> or at minimum preserving the error variant name in the string.

title: Untyped NIC state field — Option<String> should be NicState enum
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/network.rs, line 228
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs, line 155

**Current code:**
- API: `pub state: Option<String>` in Nic struct
- CLI: `"state" => nic.state == value` — raw string comparison

**Problem:** NIC state has known finite values (provisioning, running, stopped) but is modeled as `Option<String>`, losing type safety. The CLI does unvalidated string comparisons.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `NicState` enum in cloudapi-api types:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum NicState {
    Provisioning,
    Running,
    Stopped,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```
Then change `Nic.state` to `Option<NicState>`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added NicState enum, updated Nic.state field type

title: Minor SSH silent fallbacks: port tag and HOME directory
description:
Two minor silent fallback issues in the SSH command:

1. cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/ssh.rs:130-132 — If the tritoncli.ssh.port tag contains a non-numeric value (e.g., 'abc'), .parse::<u16>().ok() silently discards the parse error and falls back to port 22. The user set a custom port tag and has no idea it's being ignored.

2. cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/ssh.rs:281 — std::env::var("HOME").unwrap_or_else(|_| "/tmp".to_string()) falls back to /tmp when HOME is unset. The ControlPath will point to /tmp/.triton/tmp/ which may have unintended side effects and doesn't match the config_dir() resolution logic.

Fix: emit warnings for invalid port tags and use dirs::home_dir() consistently.

title: node-triton patch: emit-payload gaps for disk/vlan delete
description:
Tests affected: payload-disk-delete, payload-vlan-delete
Node exits 1 with empty output for these commands in emit-payload mode - the node-triton patch doesn't handle delete code paths for disks and VLANs.
Rust correctly emits DELETE payloads.
Fix: Update the node-triton emit-payload patch to handle disk delete and vlan delete subcommands.

title: P1: Image list flags missing X and + (S flag conflates two meanings)
description:
node-triton computes 5 flag types: I (has origin), P (public), X (non-active state), + (shared by you - owner matches account), S (shared with you - account in ACL). Rust computes only 3: I (origin), S (any ACL present), P (public). Missing X and + flags; S conflates two distinct meanings.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:467-480
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_image/do_list.js:129-147

Fix: Add X flag for non-active state. Differentiate + vs S by comparing image owner to current account UUID.
Test: Add fixture-based test for flag computation with various ACL/owner combinations.

title: P2: rbac apply dev-mode generates ed25519 keys vs node-triton RSA-4096
description:
node-triton generates RSA-4096 keys (ssh-keygen -t rsa -m PEM -b 4096). Rust generates ed25519 keys. Modern and better, but older SmartOS/CloudAPI may not support ed25519.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:1142-1143
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/rbac.js:810-811

Fix: Low priority — ed25519 is reasonable modernization. Document the change. Consider RSA fallback for older infra.

title: update_image doc comment missing Share and Unshare actions
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/lib.rs:733-739, the update_image doc comment lists 4 actions but ImageAction enum has 6. The Share and Unshare actions are missing from the documentation.
close_reason: Added share/unshare note to update_image doc

title: Debug format anti-pattern on RoleTagResource in role_tags.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role_tags.rs, line 25
Uses format\!("{:?}", resource_type) as fallback in user-facing error message.

Current code:
    let type_name = resource_type
        .to_possible_value()
        .map(|v| v.get_name().to_string())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| format\!("{:?}", resource_type));

Since RoleTagResource derives ValueEnum, to_possible_value() should never return None.
The {:?} fallback would produce "Instance" instead of "instance" if triggered.

Suggested fix - simplify since ValueEnum guarantees a value:
    let type_name = resource_type
        .to_possible_value()
        .expect("ValueEnum always has a value")
        .get_name()
        .to_string();

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli

title: Silent RSA fallback for unknown SSH key algorithms
description: libs/triton-auth/src/signature.rs:47,50,64,67 - Unknown algorithm types silently map to KeyType::Rsa, producing incorrect HTTP Signature headers. Unknown ECDSA curves also silently default to P-256. Should return an error or log a warning.

title: ErrorResponse.code is untyped String requiring hardcoded string matching
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/common.rs:25-35, ErrorResponse.code is String. Consumer code (e.g. errors.rs ResourceNotFoundError) must match on string literals like ResourceNotFound, violating the No Hardcoded Enum Strings rule. Consider an ErrorCode enum with #[serde(other)] fallback, or at minimum typed constants.

title: CI coverage job has no needs: dependency on validation
description: .github/workflows/ci.yml:41-72 — Coverage job runs in parallel with validation jobs (no needs: dependency). Coverage could pass while validation fails, giving a false sense of health. Fix: add needs: [validation] to the coverage job.
close_reason: wontfix: CI file has only one job (build-test-validate, 40 lines). No separate coverage job exists — the described lines 41-72 don't exist. Issue is not applicable to current codebase.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1B: build config & CI review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: P0: Multi-target lifecycle commands exit on first error, skipping remaining instances
description:
lifecycle.rs start/stop/reboot iterate targets with `for instance in &args.instances` and use `?` to propagate errors immediately. If `instance start inst1 inst2 inst3` fails on inst1, inst2 and inst3 are never attempted. node-triton attempts ALL targets and collects errors.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/lifecycle.rs:56-77 (start), :80-101 (stop), :104-126 (reboot)

Fix: Collect errors in a Vec, attempt all targets, report all failures at the end, exit non-zero if any failed.
Test: Mock server returning 404 for first instance, 202 for others — verify all are attempted.
close_reason: Fixed: lifecycle and delete commands now collect errors across all instances instead of exiting on first failure

title: Instance list silently drops image list API failures
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs:318 — 'Err(_) => HashMap::new()' silently discards all errors from the images API call. If authentication is broken or the API is down, the image names column in instance list output will simply be blank with no indication of the failure. Fix: emit a warning to stderr: eprintln\!("warning: failed to fetch images: {}", e)

title: Stringly-typed fields where typed enums exist
description:
Three locations use String fields where corresponding typed enums are defined in the same codebase:

1. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs:250-268 — MigrationProgressEntry.phase and .state are String, but MigrationPhase and MigrationState enums exist in the same file (lines 156-182). Both enums have #[serde(other)] Unknown for forward compatibility.

2. apis/vmapi-api/src/types/jobs.rs:45 — ListJobsQuery.execution is Option<String> but JobExecution enum exists at line 63 with #[serde(other)] Unknown.

3. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs:167 — ChangefeedMessage.changed_resource_id is String but these are always VM UUIDs and should be typed as Uuid.

Fix: Replace String fields with their typed enum/Uuid equivalents.

title: Private key files written with default permissions
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs:586-587 and :728-729 - Docker/CMON private keys written with fs::write which uses umask-dependent permissions. Should explicitly set 0o600 on Unix for private key material.

title: RBAC role values not URL-encoded in create_machine
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:378 - The create_machine method joins role names with roles.join(",") without URL-encoding, while auth.rs correctly uses urlencoding::encode(). Role names with special characters produce malformed URLs and signature mismatches. This is a consequence of create_machine bypassing the Progenitor pre-hook.

title: P3: Instance create output missing image/package details
description:
node-triton prints 'Creating instance NAME (UUID, IMAGE@VERSION, PACKAGE)' including resolved image name@version and package. Rust prints 'Creating instance NAME (SHORT_UUID)' — omits resolved image and package names.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/create.rs:304
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_instance/do_create.js:372-374

Fix: Include resolved image name@version and package name in create output.

title: Volume create default size calculation may use wrong units
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs:278-283 - Comment says Convert GB to MB but multiplies by 1024. Unclear whether API s.size is in GB or GiB or already MiB. If already MiB, multiplication produces volume 1024x too large. Needs verification against actual API.

title: No tests for GetMachineError 410 Gone recovery logic
description: TypedClient::get_machine in clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs (lines 974-1013) implements special handling for HTTP 410 Gone responses where the server returns a Machine body instead of an Error body. The recovery logic catches Error::InvalidResponsePayload, tries to parse as Machine, and validates state == Deleted. There are no unit or integration tests for any GetMachineError variant (NotFound, JsonParse, UnexpectedRecovery). If the InvalidResponsePayload variant name changes in a Progenitor update, or if the MachineState::Deleted comparison breaks, the 410 recovery would silently fail.

title: vmapi-api has zero deserialization tests
description: apis/vmapi-api/ — The vmapi-api crate has no deserialization tests at all. Type definitions cannot be validated against real VMAPI responses. Fix: add response fixtures from actual VMAPI endpoints and round-trip deserialization tests.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2C: vmapi-api types review. Rated MEDIUM — known coverage gap.

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on IssueSort
description:
File: apis/bugview-api/src/lib.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, JsonSchema`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`
**Has:** Custom `Display` impl and `as_str()` method

**Problem:** `IssueSort` is used in bugview-cli as a value_enum argument but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch in bugview-client.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=bugview-api && make package-build PACKAGE=bugview-cli`

title: Insecure password generation in RBAC apply
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:857-875 — generate_password() uses SystemTime::now().as_nanos() as its sole entropy source, which is trivially predictable. Used for newly created RBAC sub-users. Fix: use uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string() (uses OS entropy) or add getrandom crate.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated CRITICAL — insecure entropy source for passwords.

title: Untyped disk state field — Option<String> should be DiskState enum
description:
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs, line 143

**Current code:**
- `disk.state.as_deref().unwrap_or("unknown")` — raw string with hardcoded fallback

**Problem:** Disk state is `Option<String>` in the Progenitor-generated types but has known finite values. The CLI falls back to a hardcoded `"unknown"` string.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `DiskState` enum in cloudapi-api types (or reuse `SnapshotState` if the values overlap), then update the Disk struct to use it.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added DiskState enum to replace Option<String>

title: P2: rbac role-tags set/remove/clear missing confirmation prompts
description:
node-triton prompts 'Set role tags on <type> "<id>"? [y/n]' (skippable with -y). Rust executes immediately without confirmation. Destructive role-tag operations (especially clear which removes all tags) have no safety net.

Files: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role_tags.rs:130 (set), :272 (remove), :294 (clear)
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_rbac/do_role_tags.js:257-271

Fix: Add -y/--yes flag; prompt for confirmation when not in non-interactive mode.

title: No tests for add_auth_headers (RBAC role injection, X-Act-As, Accept-Version)
description:
clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/auth.rs — The add_auth_headers function handles RBAC role injection into query parameters (before signing, to prevent parameter tampering), X-Act-As header for operator masquerading, and Accept-Version header. None of these code paths have any tests.

If the RBAC as-role query parameter construction breaks (e.g. roles not URL-encoded, appended after signing), an attacker could tamper with role parameters. If X-Act-As or Accept-Version header injection regresses, operators lose masquerading capability silently.

Fix: Create tests that construct reqwest::Request objects, call add_auth_headers with various AuthConfig settings, and verify the resulting headers and URL query parameters.

title: P2: Missing fixture files for disk, NIC, snapshot, key, fwrule resource types
description:
No JSON fixtures exist for: disks, NICs, snapshots, SSH keys, firewall rules. These resource types can only be tested with API access (integration tests). Other resource types (machine, image, network, volume, package) have fixtures.

Directory: cli/triton-cli/tests/fixtures/

Fix: Add JSON fixture files for each missing resource type, then add offline tests using them.

title: node-triton patch: trailing empty objects in tag/metadata set
description:
Tests affected: payload-tag-set, payload-tag-replace, payload-meta-set
The node-triton emit-payload patch emits trailing {} empty objects in the request array for these commands. This is a patch artifact, not a Rust bug.
Fix: Update the node-triton emit-payload patch to suppress empty trailing objects for tag and metadata code paths.
close_reason: Fixed in 4edc042: jq filter select(.method) drops trailing {} objects

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on MigrationState (vmapi-api)
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/migrations.rs

**Current variants:** Begin, Estimate, Sync, Paused, Switch, Aborted, RolledBack, Successful, Failed, Running
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If VMAPI adds new migration states, deserialization will fail. CloudAPI MigrationState correctly has `#[serde(other)] Unknown` but the VMAPI counterpart does not.

**Suggested fix:** Add `#[serde(other)] Unknown` variant to `vmapi_api::MigrationState`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`

title: Beads/type-safety-audit cross-reference missing in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md mentions both Beads issue tracking and the type-safety-audit skill but does not cross-reference them. Contributors may not know that type safety findings should be tracked as Beads issues. Fix: add cross-references between the sections.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: triton rbac role create: member/policy format differs from node-triton
description:
Comparison test ID: payload-rbac-role-create

Node sends members as full objects and policies as objects:
  "members": [{"default": false, "id": "testuser", "type": "subuser"}]
  "policies": [{"name": "testpolicy"}]

Rust sends them as simple strings:
  "members": ["testuser"]
  "policies": ["testpolicy"]

The API may accept both formats, but we should match node-triton's wire format for compatibility.

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_rbac.rs (or similar)

title: Fragile is_check arg detection in openapi-manager
description: openapi-manager/src/main.rs:118 - std::env::args().any(|a| a == "check") scans ALL args including binary path. Should restrict to subcommand position with args().nth(1).as_deref() == Some("check").

title: vmapi-cli has zero tests
description: cli/vmapi-cli/tests/ directory does not exist. Not even basic help/version smoke tests. Any regression in argument parsing, output formatting, or subcommand routing would be undetected. Should add at minimum offline tests following triton-cli pattern.

title: triton instance: missing block_size in disks, extra flags field
description:
Comparison test IDs: instance-list-json, instance-get-json, instance-get
- Rust is missing block_size field in disk objects (Node has block_size: 8192)
- Rust has extra flags: "-" field on one instance that Node doesn't include
- Likely source: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs (Disk struct), cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance.rs
- Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_instances.rs

Diff (instance-get-json):
-      "block_size": 8192,
       "boot": true,
+    "flags": "-",

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VolumeState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs, lines 25-30

**Current variants:** Creating, Ready, Failed, Deleting
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If CloudAPI adds new volume states in the future, deserialization will fail with an error instead of gracefully falling back. Other state enums (MachineState, SnapshotState, MigrationState, MigrationPhase) correctly use the `#[serde(other)] Unknown` pattern.

**Suggested fix:**
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum VolumeState {
    Creating,
    Ready,
    Failed,
    Deleting,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`

title: Add positional key=value filter support to volume list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton volume list` (e.g., `triton volumes name=mydata`). The Rust CLI currently has no filter support at all (no --flag filters either).

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add --flag filter fields to `VolumeListArgs` for each supported filter
2. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]`
3. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into args fields
4. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list
5. Apply filters to the API request (check what the ListVolumes API endpoint supports as query params)

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_volume/do_list.js`): name, size, state, owner, type

**Note**: node-triton uses `jsonPredFromKv()` for volume filtering (server-side JSON predicate). The Rust implementation should check whether the CloudAPI ListVolumes endpoint supports query parameter filtering directly.

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: None (only has TableFormatArgs for output formatting)

title: Profile create/delete workflow tests
description: Only read-only profile tests exist (profile get env, profile list). No tests for profile create or profile delete workflows. Requires careful temp dir management to avoid affecting real profile configs.

title: write_ssh_mpint panics on empty input (usize underflow)
description: libs/triton-auth/src/legacy_pem.rs:532 — write_ssh_mpint performs a subtraction that underflows on empty input, causing a panic. Fix: add an early return or error for empty byte slices.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM — panic on edge case.

title: Restify conversion skill not mentioned in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not mention the restify-conversion skill, which is available for migrating Node.js Restify services to Rust. This skill is useful for contributors doing migration work. Fix: add a reference to the restify-conversion skill in the relevant AGENTS.md section.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: client-generator check hides I/O errors as STALE with unwrap_or_default()
description:
client-generator/src/main.rs:233 — The cmd_check function uses unwrap_or_default() when reading the actual generated file. If the file exists but cannot be read (permission denied, I/O error), the empty string is compared against the expected code, always producing a misleading 'STALE' result. Developer re-runs make clients-generate, code regenerates identically, CI still fails.

Fix: Handle missing-file explicitly with Err(NotFound) => String::new(), and propagate other I/O errors with an explicit error message.

title: scan_ssh_dir_for_key silently swallows key load errors
description:
libs/triton-auth/src/key_loader.rs:259-273 — The let-chain that scans ~/.ssh/ for matching keys silently discards errors from try_exists, load_legacy_from_file, and public_key_blob. If a key file exists but fails to load (permission denied, corrupt PEM, encrypted without passphrase), the function silently moves on. User sees 'No key with fingerprint ... found' with no hint that the key was found but could not be loaded.

Fix: Restructure the let-chain into explicit match arms with tracing::debug logging for each failure reason.

title: Snapshot.state is Option<String> instead of typed enum
description: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/vms.rs:418-428 - Every other state field in vmapi-api uses a typed enum with #[serde(other)] Unknown, but Snapshot.state uses Option<String>. Should define a SnapshotState enum.

title: CLI compat: support field=value positional syntax for update commands
description: node-triton uses 'image update UUID name=new-name version=2.0.0', 'fwrule update UUID rule=...', and 'vlan update ID name=...' with positional field=value pairs. Rust CLI uses named --flags instead. Affects payload tests: payload-image-update, payload-fwrule-update, payload-vlan-update.

title: RBAC subcommand help and existence tests
description: The entire RBAC subsystem (~20 subcommands: rbac info, apply, reset, user list/get/create/delete/update, role list/get/create/delete/update, policy list/get/create/delete, key, accesskey, role-tags) has zero test coverage. At minimum, add help/existence tests in cli_subcommands.rs matching the pattern used for every other command group.
close_reason: Added 41 RBAC help/existence tests to cli_subcommands.rs

title: Error schema patching should use Progenitor with_patch instead of patched/ directory
description:
Files:
- openapi-manager/src/transforms.rs (post-generation transform)
- clients/internal/cloudapi-client/build.rs:14-16 (reads from patched/)
- openapi-specs/patched/cloudapi-api.json (patched spec)

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns errors as `{code, message, request_id?}` but Dropshot generates `{error_code, message, request_id}` (required). Current workaround maintains a separate `openapi-specs/patched/` directory with a post-generation transform in openapi-manager.

**Suggested fix:** Use `with_patch("Error", &error_patch)` in cloudapi-client build.rs to rename `error_code` → `code` and make `request_id` optional, then read from `openapi-specs/generated/` directly. Eliminates the patched/ directory entirely.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-client && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated spec = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched spec = actual Node.js wire format. The diff is valuable migration documentation. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Won't fix: keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched = actual Node.js wire format. The diff documents the migration gap. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

title: Duplicate MigrationPhase and SnapshotState enums across cloudapi-api and vmapi-api
description: MigrationPhase (cloudapi misc.rs:176, vmapi migrations.rs:89) and SnapshotState (cloudapi machine_resources.rs:62, vmapi vms.rs:424) have identical variants in both crates. Should use cross-crate import pattern (like Brand) to prevent drift. MigrationState and MigrationAction correctly differ and should stay separate.

title: transform_create_machine_body silently no-ops on body parse failure
description:
clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:248-256 — The pre-hook that flattens tags/metadata for create_machine silently returns if the body is missing, non-bytes, or non-JSON. If Progenitor changes its body format (e.g. streaming instead of buffered), as_bytes() returns None and the body transformation silently stops. User's tags and metadata are lost with zero indication.

Fix: Add tracing::warn on early returns in transform_create_machine_body. A missing body or non-JSON body on a create_machine call is always suspicious.

title: Workspace dependency inconsistencies in triton-cli and triton-auth
description: cli/triton-cli/Cargo.toml: thiserror, tracing-subscriber, uuid, chrono, tempfile use direct version strings instead of workspace = true. The uuid pin to 1.16 vs workspace 1 is most notable. libs/triton-auth/Cargo.toml: thiserror = 2.0 should be workspace = true.

title: human_size_from_bytes array index panic on extreme values
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/info.rs:101-103 — human_size_from_bytes uses an array index computed from the value magnitude. Extremely large byte values cause an out-of-bounds array index panic. Fix: clamp the index to the array bounds or handle the overflow case.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3C: triton-cli infrastructure commands review. Rated HIGH — panic on edge case input.

title: No serde tests for Package struct with DiskSize untagged enum
description: The Package struct in apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs has tricky DiskSize untagged enum (u64 or remaining string), cross-crate Brand, flexible_disk rename override, and role-tag hyphenated field. CLI tests validate fixture JSON but there is no dedicated serde test like other major types (Machine, Image, Volume, Network).

title: 5 identical stale TODO comments in resolver functions
description:
Five identical '// TODO: should we add verification here that node-triton didn't have?' comments appear across resolver functions with no actionable detail:

- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/fwrule.rs:447
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:61
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs:568
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/vlan.rs:227
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/common.rs:15

These don't specify what verification is being considered (existence check? format validation?). A newcomer cannot act on them. Either convert to specific bead issues describing the exact behavior needed, or remove them.
