title: payload-image-copy: Node resolves image+datacenters, Rust sends POST directly
description: In the image copy payload test, Node does GET /images/<uuid> then GET /datacenters (to resolve the target DC) before failing (exit 1). Rust sends POST /images directly (exit 0). Node creates a separate client targeting the destination DC, which the emit-payload fake server can't handle. The Rust implementation sends the copy request directly to the current DC's API. Both approaches may be valid but produce different payloads.

title: No write timeout on SSH agent socket
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:183-188 — Write operations to the SSH agent socket have no timeout. A hung agent socket causes the CLI to block indefinitely. Fix: set a write timeout on the UnixStream.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: RBAC key fetch silently swallows API errors in rbac info --all
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:238-240, if let Ok(keys) = keys_result during rbac info --all makes failed key fetches indistinguishable from users with zero keys. Security-relevant: an RBAC audit tool hiding errors produces a false sense of security. Should log failures and show ERROR or ? instead of 0 key(s).

title: No end-to-end test for CertGenerator::generate()
description: libs/triton-auth/src/certgen.rs (660 lines) has 10 unit tests for DER encoding helpers, but no test that exercises CertGenerator::generate() — the actual certificate generation flow. This module generates X.509 certificates for Docker TLS setup, a security-sensitive operation. The unit tests verify encoding primitives but do not verify that a generated certificate is actually valid or parseable by standard TLS libraries. Fix: add a test that generates a certificate with CertGenerator and verifies the result parses as valid X.509.

title: Hardcoded enum string "created" in cli_snapshots.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_snapshots.rs
Lines: 223, 279

**Current code:**
- Line 223: `assert_eq!(snap.state, "created")`
- Line 279: `assert_eq!(snap.state, "created")`

**Problem:** Test assertions compare against hardcoded `"created"` string instead of `SnapshotState::Created` typed enum variant.

**Suggested fix:** Use `SnapshotState::Created` in assertions. May require changing test struct `state` field from String to SnapshotState.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Fixed: state field now uses SnapshotState enum

title: NIC list filter uses string comparison for state instead of typed enum
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs:155
Line: `"state" => nic.state == value,`

**Problem:**
The NIC list filter converts NicState to a String via enum_to_display() at line 120, then compares the stringified state against the user-provided filter value at line 155. This bypasses type safety:
- An invalid state value (typo, wrong case like 'Running' vs 'running') silently filters out all results without error
- No compile-time guarantee that filter values stay in sync with NicState variants

**Other list commands do this correctly:**
- instance/list.rs uses parse_serde_enum() to parse the filter string into a typed MachineState
- volume.rs uses parse_serde_enum() to parse into typed VolumeState/VolumeType
- image.rs uses parse_serde_enum() to parse into typed ImageState/ImageType

**Suggested fix:**
Parse the user-provided state value into a NicState enum before comparing:
```rust
// In the filter section, parse 'value' into NicState first:
"state" => {
    let target: NicState = parse_serde_enum(value)
        .map_err(|_| anyhow!("Invalid state: {}", value))?;
    enum_to_display(&nic.state) == enum_to_display(&target)
}
```
Or better: apply filters on the typed Nic struct before converting to NicOutput.

**Verification:** After fix, `triton inst nic list <id> state=INVALID` should return an error instead of silently returning zero results.

title: Config load error masked as no profile configured in rbac apply
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:354-363, Config::load().await.ok() converts corrupt config file errors into None, producing misleading message 'requires a configured profile' when the real problem is an unreadable config. Should match on Result and surface the underlying error.

title: resource_state_typed() doc claims it can return None but it cannot
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs:196-201, doc says returns None for unparseable states, but VmState::FromStr maps all unknowns to Ok(VmState::Unknown) so it always returns Some. Doc should be updated to reflect reality, noting Option is preserved for forward compat if FromStr ever becomes fallible.

title: Brand enum missing #[serde(other)] catch-all
description: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs:44-57 — Brand enum has no catch-all variant for unknown values. Cascades to cloudapi Machine.brand and Package.brand. New brands from the server will cause deserialization failures. Fix: add #[serde(other)] Unknown variant following the changefeed.rs pattern.
